description: Suitable for Band 6.0–7.0 target. More complex data sets and essay structures requiring nuanced arguments.

task1.title: Line Graph — CO₂ Emissions
task1.description: The line graph below shows the CO₂ emissions per capita in four countries between 1990 and 2020.
tips: Describe trends over time, not point-by-point
Use trend language: rose sharply, declined steadily, plateaued
Make at least one comparison between countries
End with the final position of each line

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Paraphrase — what the graph shows.

structure.label: Overview
structure.desc: Highest emitter + most notable change.

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Countries 1 & 2 — trends over time.

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: Countries 3 & 4 — trends and comparison.

task1.title: Bar Chart — University Degrees by Gender
task1.description: The bar chart below shows the percentage of men and women who completed university degrees in six subjects in 2019.
tips: Compare male vs female patterns — don't describe each bar separately
Identify subjects with the biggest gender gap
Use comparative language: 'while', 'whereas', 'in contrast'
Avoid repeating the same structure for every subject

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Paraphrase — subjects, genders, time period.

structure.label: Overview
structure.desc: Overall pattern + subject with biggest gender gap.

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Subjects where women predominate with figures.

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: Subjects where men predominate + one balanced subject.

task1.title: Two Charts — Household Expenditure
task1.description: The charts below show how households in one country spent their income in 1980 and 2020.
tips: Always compare the two time periods — don't describe each chart separately
Focus on the most significant changes
Use language of change: 'rose significantly', 'fell by', 'remained relatively stable'
Include at least one figure from each chart

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: What both charts show — spending over two periods.

structure.label: Overview
structure.desc: Most notable changes between 1980 and 2020.

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Categories that increased — with figures for both years.

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: Categories that decreased or stayed the same.

task2.title: Discussion Essay — Remote Work
task2.question: Remote working has become increasingly common. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of this trend and give your own opinion.
tips: Present BOTH sides before giving your opinion
Use a clear topic sentence for each paragraph
Avoid personal anecdotes — use general examples
Your opinion in the conclusion must match your essay

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Topic intro + outline (discuss both + opinion).

structure.label: Advantages
structure.desc: 2 advantages with explanation and example.

structure.label: Disadvantages
structure.desc: 2 disadvantages with explanation and example.

structure.label: Conclusion
structure.desc: Summary + your overall opinion.

task2.title: Problem-Solution — Childhood Obesity
task2.question: The rate of obesity in children is increasing in many countries. What are the main causes of this problem, and what solutions could governments and individuals take?
tips: Separate causes and solutions clearly
Each paragraph = one main idea, fully developed
Use modal verbs: could, should, might, would
Be specific — name actual policies or behaviours

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: State the problem + your approach (causes & solutions).

structure.label: Causes
structure.desc: 2 main causes — unhealthy diet, sedentary lifestyle.

structure.label: Solutions
structure.desc: Government + individual solutions linked to causes.

structure.label: Conclusion
structure.desc: Summarise and restate key points.

keyPhrases: One of the primary causes is …
At an individual level, people should …

task2.title: Two-Part Question — Space Exploration
task2.question: Some people think that space exploration is a waste of money and these funds should be used for other purposes. Why do some people think this? Do you agree or disagree?
tips: Answer BOTH parts of the question explicitly
Part 1: explain others' reasons without giving your view
Part 2: give your own view with clear reasoning
Don't combine both parts into one paragraph

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Introduce the debate + state you will address both parts.

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Reasons why people think space is wasteful (not your view).

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: Your own view — agree or disagree + reasons + example.

structure.label: Conclusion
structure.desc: Summarise both parts + restate your position.

keyPhrases: Those who oppose space funding argue that …
The benefits of space research include …
In conclusion, while I understand the concerns …
